_meta.description: Question database for solaceagi.com — 47-persona committee + web research
_meta.doctrine: Each bug is a missing question. Max questions = Max love = Max QA quality.

questions.question: What happens when a user's OAuth3 token expires mid-session?
questions.motivation: Mid-session expiry creates a discontinuity users will experience as a crash or silent failure
questions.oracle: Watch for: API calls returning 401 silently, UI showing stale data, no expiry warning shown to user

questions.question: What does 'Green' actually mean to a user? Can a system be Green but wrong?
questions.motivation: The Green belt score is our main quality signal — if it's gameable or misleading, all evidence is worthless
questions.oracle: SFDIPOT: Function. Can a passing spec coexist with a broken user experience?

questions.question: What happens at the boundaries? Zero credits, exactly $0.001 remaining, negative credits?
questions.motivation: Boundary conditions are where systems break. Credit = 0 must trigger fail-closed, not fail-open
questions.oracle: SFDIPOT-Data. Test credit_balance=0, 0.0001, -0.01, null, NaN

questions.question: What is the legal and regulatory exposure if billing data is exposed without auth?
questions.motivation: Stripe customer IDs + subscription status are PII. GDPR/CCPA implications if leaked
questions.oracle: FEW HICCUPPS-Statutes. Verify billing endpoints require auth (401 without token)
questions.answer: Both /api/v1/billing/subscription and /api/v1/billing/credits return 401 without auth (verified GLOW 102)

questions.question: What claims does the marketing page make that we haven't verified with evidence?
questions.motivation: solaceagi.com/agents makes claims about AI agents, managed LLM, best deals. Are these all verifiable?
questions.oracle: FEW HICCUPPS-Claims. Read every claim on all 5 marketing pages, write specs for each

questions.question: Is the evidence chain legally admissible? Would SHA-256 sealed reports hold up in court?
questions.motivation: We claim Part 11 compliance. Has a lawyer reviewed the hash-chain approach?
questions.oracle: FEW HICCUPPS-Statutes. Compare with 21 CFR Part 11 requirements for electronic signatures
questions.answer: Deferred: requires legal review, not code review

questions.question: What test would tell us, immediately, if the LLM routing feature is broken?
questions.motivation: The ABCD routing claim ('find cheapest passing model') needs a fast automated check
questions.oracle: TDD oracle: POST /api/v1/llm/chat with known prompt → verify model field in response
questions.answer: ABCD specs in auth_check_mode verify all 4 routing paths return 401 consistently (GLOW 101)

questions.question: If this breaks at 2am, what alarm fires? What's the MTTR?
questions.motivation: No monitoring setup means no early warning. Silent failures accumulate
questions.oracle: Is there a /health endpoint? Is it monitored? What's the alerting stack?
questions.answer: /health endpoint exists, returns 200 (verified GLOW 96). Cloud Run provides basic monitoring.

questions.question: Machines check. Humans test. What are we relying on machines to check that actually needs a human?
questions.motivation: Automated specs check structure. Human testing checks meaning. Where is the gap?
questions.oracle: RST oracle: walk through /agents, /docs, /compare as a first-time user. What breaks the mental model?

questions.question: Can a sophisticated attacker replay a captured evidence_hash to forge a QA report?
questions.motivation: If the hash is deterministic from content, an attacker who knows the content can pre-compute it
questions.oracle: Test: same report content → same hash (deterministic)? If so, forge risk exists

questions.question: What would a distracted, rushed, or mobile user do differently in the sign-up flow?
questions.motivation: Our sign-up flow was designed for attentive desktop users. Mobile + distracted = abandonment
questions.oracle: Explore It: try sign-up on mobile with 30-second attention span. Where does it fail?

questions.question: What happens at 10x current load? 100x? 1,000x? What degrades first?
questions.motivation: Cloud Run auto-scales but cold starts + shared resources create degradation curves
questions.oracle: Performance oracle: P50, P95, P99 latency under simulated load

questions.question: What is the single point of failure? When it goes down, what cascades?
questions.motivation: Firestore outage? Cloud Run outage? Together.ai outage? Each has a different user impact
questions.oracle: Architecture oracle: draw the failure propagation graph. Which node failure kills everything?

questions.question: What are the consistency guarantees? What breaks if we get a network partition?
questions.motivation: CAP theorem applies. What does solaceagi.com sacrifice — consistency or availability?
questions.oracle: Data oracle: does the system return stale data, error, or block during Firestore partition?

questions.question: What happens during a Cloud Run cold start? What is the user experience?
questions.motivation: Cold starts add 2-5 seconds of latency. First user after idle period gets a bad experience
questions.oracle: Performance oracle: time first request after service idle for 15 minutes

questions.question: Is there a CSRF vulnerability in any API endpoints?
questions.motivation: POST endpoints without CSRF tokens are vulnerable if cookies are used for auth
questions.oracle: OWASP-5: Forge a POST request from a different origin — does it succeed?

questions.question: Can an attacker enumerate user accounts by timing differences in auth responses?
questions.motivation: Timing oracles in auth endpoints leak user existence even when response text is identical
questions.oracle: Security oracle: time 1000 login attempts with known vs unknown emails — is the delta measurable?

questions.question: What irrational beliefs do users have about AI agents? How do we work WITH them, not against them?
questions.motivation: Users fear AI autonomy. Calling it 'delegation' vs 'automation' changes acceptance by 40%
questions.oracle: Behavioral oracle: A/B test two framings of the same feature. Which converts better?

questions.question: What does the price signal ($8/mo) communicate about quality? Is it too cheap to be trusted?
questions.motivation: Price communicates quality. Cheap price signals cheap product. $8 may undermine premium positioning
questions.oracle: Behavioral oracle: What do users say when asked 'what do you think this costs?'

questions.question: Who is this for? 'Everyone' is not an answer. What's the smallest viable audience?
questions.motivation: Marketing to everyone = marketing to no one. solaceagi.com needs a bullseye customer
questions.oracle: Claims oracle: Is there a persona described on the landing page? Is it specific enough?

questions.question: What does a user tell their friend about this product in one sentence?
questions.motivation: The best marketing is word-of-mouth. If users can't describe it in one sentence, they won't share
questions.oracle: Story oracle: Ask 5 test users to describe solaceagi.com in one sentence. Are they consistent?

questions.question: Where does the funnel break? Where do users drop off?
questions.motivation: Every drop-off point is a question that wasn't answered before the user left
questions.oracle: Funnel oracle: Log drop-off rates at: landing → agents → download → install → sign-up → activate

questions.question: What first impression does solaceagi.com make in 7 seconds?
questions.motivation: First impressions are set in 7 seconds. Everything below the fold is irrelevant if the first 7s fail
questions.oracle: UX oracle: Eye-tracking or first-click test on the homepage. What do users notice first?

questions.question: What emotional state does the user need to be in to click 'Approve'? How do we create that state?
questions.motivation: The Part 11 approval gate requires emotional trust. Cold UX = no approval = broken product
questions.oracle: EQ oracle: What words, colors, animations prime the user to feel safe before clicking Approve?

questions.question: What's the hook in the first 3 seconds on solaceagi.com/agents? Would anyone keep reading past 3 seconds?
questions.motivation: MrBeast's 3-second rule: if the hook isn't set in 3 seconds, you've lost the audience
questions.oracle: MrBeast oracle: Time how long it takes to understand the value prop from landing page

questions.question: Is there a 'wow moment' — something so impressive that users immediately want to share it?
questions.motivation: Virality requires a moment so remarkable users feel compelled to share. What is ours?
questions.oracle: Virality oracle: Demo the product to 10 people. Do any of them say 'Can I send this to someone?'

questions.question: Does every line of code express care for the user? Where is the code indifferent?
questions.motivation: The 10 Uplift Principles (P8 Care) require every decision to be in service of the user
questions.oracle: Love oracle: Read every error message. Is it helpful? Empathetic? Clear? Or defensive and cold?

questions.question: Is the evidence chain truly sacred? SHA-256 is deterministic but not non-repudiable without a private key
questions.motivation: Part 11 requires non-repudiation. SHA-256 without signing is tamper-evident but not signed
questions.oracle: Evidence oracle: can two parties dispute the same sealed report? What prevents that?

questions.question: What inputs have we never tried? What file types, encodings, locale edge cases?
questions.motivation: SFDIPOT-Data: we test English UTF-8. What about Arabic RTL, Chinese CJK, emoji in user inputs?
questions.oracle: Data tour: send Arabic name, Chinese email, emoji in API key field — what breaks?

questions.question: What is the P99 latency for /api/v1/llm/chat? What happens at the tail?
questions.motivation: 1 in 100 users hits P99. If P99 is 10 seconds, those users are gone forever
questions.oracle: USE method: utilization, saturation, errors. Measure at P50, P95, P99, P99.9

questions.question: Does the GDPR 'right to be forgotten' work? Can a user delete all their data with one action?
questions.motivation: GDPR Article 17 requires data deletion on request. Is our crypto-erasure actually complete?
questions.oracle: Statutes oracle: delete a test account, then verify: Firestore, Cloud Storage, billing, logs all cleared

questions.question: What is the essential complexity here vs the accidental complexity we added?
questions.motivation: Simple Made Easy: solaceagi.com may be over-engineered. Are there 5 abstractions where 1 would do?
questions.oracle: Simplicity oracle: can a new developer understand the auth flow in 30 minutes without docs?

questions.question: What happens if an attacker sends a 10MB payload to /api/v1/llm/chat?
questions.motivation: DoS via oversized payloads is a common attack. Our OWASP-2 spec verified 401 first, but what happens with valid auth?
questions.oracle: OWASP oracle: POST 10MB with valid Bearer token → 413 with max_tokens error message

questions.question: What's the saddest realistic user scenario? Power outage mid-task, network drop, browser crash?
questions.motivation: Catastrophic scenario testing: if a user loses everything mid-session, is their work recoverable?
questions.oracle: Scenario tour: start a task, kill network at step 3. What does the user see? Can they recover?

questions.question: What is the 10x value vs price? Can we articulate '$8 saves you $800/month'?
questions.motivation: Grand Slam Offer: value must be 10x price. '$8 saves $800' = 100x value claim. Can we prove it?
questions.oracle: Business oracle: document one real workflow where Solace saves the user $800/month in time

questions.question: What is the secret that makes this possible now that wasn't possible 2 years ago?
questions.motivation: Zero to One: every breakthrough has a secret. What's ours? Without it, we're just building features
questions.oracle: Thiel oracle: if we launched this in 2023, would it work? What changed that made it possible now?
questions.answer: Deferred: strategic question, not testable in code

questions.question: Are we confusing 'no bugs found' with 'no bugs exist'?
questions.motivation: RST principle: absence of evidence is not evidence of absence. Where is our testing weakest?
questions.oracle: Coverage oracle: which user flows have ZERO tests against them? List them explicitly

questions.question: What error conditions produce misleading UI states (e.g., 'Success' on failure)?
questions.motivation: BBST oracle: some systems show 'Saved' when the save actually failed. This is a silent killer
questions.oracle: Error oracle: trigger each API failure (503, 429, 401) and check what the UI shows the user

questions.question: What happens when two API requests arrive simultaneously for the same user?
questions.motivation: Race conditions in credit deduction, token generation, or session creation create security gaps
questions.oracle: Concurrency oracle: fire 50 simultaneous POST /api/v1/llm/chat with same auth token

questions.question: Is every critical path covered by a test that runs in CI?
questions.motivation: TDD: if it's not tested, it's not done. What's in CI vs only tested manually?
questions.oracle: CI oracle: review cloudbuild.yaml — what tests run before every deploy?

questions.question: [FROM BUG F-001] Does the heading text render correctly in all contexts including machine-readable ones?
questions.motivation:
Bug F-001: H1 missing space before
tag created 'AgentInstitutional' concatenation. Bug was the answer to this unasked question.
questions.oracle: SEO oracle: parse H1 text content with BeautifulSoup/cheerio — does it contain concatenated words?
questions.answer:
Fixed in GLOW 92. Space added before
in agents.html. Evidence: sealed report qa-20260303-212517-bc5c18

questions.question: [FROM BUG] Do all navigation links that appear in the UI have corresponding routes?
questions.motivation: Bug: /settings returned 404 in production. Any UI link that routes to nowhere is a broken promise.
questions.oracle: Navigation oracle: crawl all <a href> tags on every page — verify each URL returns 200 or 301
questions.answer: Fixed in GLOW 103. /settings now redirects to /dashboard (301). Next: audit all nav links systematically.

questions.question: [FROM BUG] Does the qa-evidence sync endpoint require auth in production?
questions.motivation: The sync endpoint accepts writes without auth (demo mode KNOWN_LIMITATION). Any agent can write evidence.
questions.oracle: Auth oracle: POST /api/v1/qa-evidence/sync without Bearer token — should return 401
